children.name: RegisterPatient
children.purpose: Register a new patient in the system
children.behavior: Extract patient details from input, validate uniqueness, store in patients data store, and return success or error
postconditions: patient record created in patients data store

inputs.name: patient_data
inputs.description: Patient registration details (name, age, contact, etc.)

outputs.name: registration_result
outputs.description: Result with success status, patient ID, and message

global_vars.description: Write new patient record to patients data store

constraints.description: Patient ID must be unique across the system
Patient data must include mandatory fields: name, age, contact

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid patient data, when RegisterPatient is called, then a new patient record is created and a unique patient ID is returned
Given duplicate patient data, when RegisterPatient is called, then an error is returned and no duplicate record is created

children.name: BookAppointment
children.purpose: Book an appointment for a registered patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and appointment details from input, verify patient exists, check slot availability, store appointment in appointments data store, and return confirmation
postconditions: appointment record created in appointments data store

inputs.name: appointment_data
inputs.description: Appointment details (patient_id, date, time, doctor, etc.)

outputs.name: booking_result
outputs.description: Result with success status, appointment ID, and message

global_vars.description: Read patient record to verify existence

constraints.description: Appointment slot must not conflict with existing appointments
Patient must be registered before booking

acceptance_criteria.description: Given a registered patient and available slot, when BookAppointment is called, then an appointment is created and a confirmation is returned
Given an unregistered patient, when BookAppointment is called, then an error is returned
Given a conflicting slot, when BookAppointment is called, then an error is returned

children.name: GetPatientRecords
children.purpose: Retrieve medical records for a patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID from input, verify patient exists, read records from patients data store (append-only), and return records

outputs.name: records_result
outputs.description: Result with success status, records list, and message

global_vars.description: Read patient records from patients data store

data_operations.description: Retrieve medical records for the given patient

constraints.description: Records are append-only and cannot be modified
Patient must be registered to retrieve records

acceptance_criteria.description: Given a registered patient, when GetPatientRecords is called, then the patient's records are returned
Given an unregistered patient, when GetPatientRecords is called, then an error is returned

children.name: UpdatePatientProfile
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and updated fields from input, verify patient exists, update profile in patients data store, and return success
postconditions: patient profile updated in patients data store

inputs.name: update_data
inputs.description: Patient ID and updated profile fields

outputs.name: update_result
outputs.description: Result with success status and message

global_vars.description: Read patient record to verify existence and write updated profile

data_operations.description: Verify patient exists and update profile fields

constraints.description: Only allowed fields can be updated (e.g., contact, address)
Patient must be registered to update profile

acceptance_criteria.description: Given a registered patient and valid update fields, when UpdatePatientProfile is called, then the patient profile is updated and success is returned
Given an unregistered patient, when UpdatePatientProfile is called, then an error is returned

governance_notes: All children operate on the same data stores (patients, appointments). RegisterPatient and UpdatePatientProfile both write to patients, so they must be serialized or use optimistic locking to avoid conflicts. BookAppointment reads patients and writes appointments. GetPatientRecords is read-only and can be parallelized. The parent must route commands based on the 'command' field in the input JSON.
